feature_costs.label: Advanced features (history, export, text input, polish mode, iteration)
Distribution, menubar, path config, and maintenance

at_a_glance: You have spent $13.57 across 21 sessions over 9 days to ship Promptly from zero to a scored 9.8/10 final review. Session 20 (2026-04-24) was the heaviest at $1.35, covering the persistent menu bar icon, quick copy, App.jsx refactoring into hooks, and the full History Panel v2 overhaul — plus the final review gate. DECISIONS.md has grown to 97KB and is now the largest single cost driver in the project; archiving pre-Phase 3 entries would trim ~$0.07–0.10 per session.

session_narratives.2026-04-18-001: The initial planning session — brainstorm, architect, and spec review. Expensive per task because these skills read and write large structured documents from scratch.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-002: The heaviest session at $1.30 — all 9 Phase 1 foundation tasks plus phase review. Eleven tasks in one run with vibe-parallel orchestration drove the cost up.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-003: Pure planning session for F-STATE — no code committed, just spec generation and review. Cost reflects reading BRIEF.md, SPEC.md, and writing detailed FEATURE_TASKS.md.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-004: F-STATE build — all 5 state machine tasks completed. Steady $0.10/task average, consistent with medium-complexity UI work.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-005: F-FIRST-RUN planning session only — no tasks committed. Feature spec and spec-review drove the cost against a growing context.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-006: Tiny session — only FRN-004 (the final first-run task) plus vibe-cost. Context overhead was relatively low.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-007: Design system implementation plus 6 bug fixes and mode dropdown fix. The large design output (10 code sections) explains the higher output token count.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-008: DECISION-004 (move recording to main window) + BUG-004 — a significant architectural refactor that rewrote several IPC channels.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-009: Vibrancy, BUG-007, and the FEATURE-001 splash screen. The splash implementation was an L-sized task that drove most of the cost.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-010: Phase 2 review + all 11 backlog fixes (BL-013 through BL-023). The review itself consumed ~80K tokens reading the full codebase.
session_narratives.2026-04-18-011: Final review gate + RFX console cleanup. A focused end-of-v1 session — two tasks, low output.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-012: Phase 4 kickoff — dark mode follow-up, language docs, TASKS update, and two feature removals (old history + language). Moderate cost for cleanup work.
session_narratives.2026-04-19-013: Session startup only plus vibe-cost. Minimal work — likely a short exploratory session with no committed tasks.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-014: FEATURE-011 Pause and Resume — all 3 tasks plus vibe-add-feature spec. Clean $0.17/task average for medium React component work.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-015: DESIGN-001 vibrancy and ambient glow — a single S-sized task. Low cost, focused change.
session_narratives.2026-04-20-016: The largest Phase 4 session at $1.17 — FEATURE-012 Iteration Mode (6 subtasks, L), plus BUG-012 PATH resolution, BUG-013/014/015 mic and whisper fixes, BUG-016 mic dialog cleanup, and FEATURE-SIGNING. DECISIONS.md at 78KB was already the primary context pressure.
session_narratives.2026-04-23-017: BUG-017 nvm PATH resolution (L, complex multi-path scan) plus FEATURE-014 Text Input (5 tasks) and its feature-014 review. First session with DECISIONS.md crossing 90KB.
session_narratives.2026-04-23-018: FEATURE-015 Polish Mode (7 tasks, includes 2 L-sized React components) plus its review, BUG-018 window lifecycle single-instance lock fix, BUG-019 dock fix (trivial), and POLISH-011 bundle compression. A wide-ranging session.
session_narratives.2026-04-24-019: FEATURE-016 Uninstaller (8 tasks: shell script + IPC + DMG extras) and FEATURE-013 Path Config Panel (8 tasks: splash.html JS + gear icon + 4 IPC handlers). Steady $0.05/task throughput.
session_narratives.2026-04-24-020: The heaviest session at $1.35 — FEATURE-017 persistent menu bar icon (PNG helpers + RAF pulse), FEATURE-018 quick copy, BUG-033 App.jsx SRP refactor into useRecording + useKeyboardShortcuts hooks, FEATURE-020 History Panel v2 (9 tasks: bookmarks, ratings, filter chips, stats bar), and the final review gate (9.8/10). DECISIONS.md at 97KB is now the dominant context overhead at ~$0.10/session.
session_narratives.2026-04-26-021: Light maintenance session — remove default Electron application menu from macOS menu bar, fix 6 history panel review issues (footer contrast, stats IIFE, chip colors), create release.sh, and run vibe-cost.

recommendations.title: DECISIONS.md is 97KB — largest cost driver in the project
recommendations.body: DECISIONS.md has grown to 97KB (~24,000 tokens) across 9 days of development. It is loaded at every session startup and referenced repeatedly during planning tasks. Entries from Phase 1 and Phase 2 are now historical artefacts — they will never be revisited. Moving D-001 through D-BUG-008 to a DECISIONS_ARCHIVE.md that is never auto-loaded would reduce per-session input overhead by roughly 15,000 tokens.

recommendations.title: Prompt caching unused — 0% cache hit rate across all 21 sessions
recommendations.body: Every session reads CLAUDE.md (21KB), ARCHITECTURE.md (24KB), and CODEBASE.md (28KB) from cold — approximately 18,000 tokens that are never cached. These three files are stable between sessions and ideal candidates for prompt caching. Enabling cache-control on the system prompt would serve these tokens at 10% of the base input cost. This is the highest-leverage optimisation available but requires Claude Code prompt caching support to be active.

forecast_narrative: The project is fully shipped — 153 tasks complete, 0 open, final review score 9.8/10. No further planned development. If new features are added in Phase 5, expect $0.80–1.20 per feature session based on the Phase 4 average of $0.73/session. The DECISIONS.md archive fix (CP-01) should be done before any Phase 5 work begins to avoid compounding the overhead.
